summary: Recent updates to the WebCodecs area of Firefox focused on improving code quality and maintainability. Developers removed some instances where an ESLint rule—designed to catch undefined variables—was previously disabled, ensuring cleaner code practices. Additionally, they updated to a defined version of the "globals" npm module, which helps in managing global variables consistently and resolved existing issues. While these changes don't directly affect users or add new features, they contribute to a more robust foundation for WebCodecs, which plays a crucial role in enabling efficient video and audio processing on the web.
